canonical_question: How do I build a services business financial model — utilization-based revenue, capacity planning, hiring triggers?
aliases: Professional services financial model with billable hours and utilization rates
Consulting firm revenue forecast with capacity planning and hiring triggers
Services business P&L model — retainer vs project revenue, gross margin targets

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: SPI Research 2025 benchmark shows billable utilization declined to 68.9% from 73.2% in 2021

constraints: Revenue must be modeled bottom-up from headcount x billable hours x rate x utilization
Utilization assumptions must differentiate by role seniority
New hire ramp-up time (2-3 months) must be modeled explicitly
FTE all-in cost is 1.25-1.4x base salary
Model must survive stress test at 60% utilization

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs a SaaS or product business financial model, not services
User needs pricing strategy guidance, not a financial model
User runs a productized service with fixed-price packages

depends_on.label: Business model definition before building financial model

related_to.label: SaaS financial model for hybrid services+product firms

inputs_needed.question: What type of services firm is being modeled?
What is the primary billing model?
What is the user's financial modeling skill level?
